perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise provided herein, the following words and terms used in this Contract shall have the meanings set forth below: Article 2 "Affiliate" means, in relation to Party A, any enterprise or other entity which, directly or indirectly, controls, or is controlled by, Party A; the term "control" meaning ownership of fifty percent (50%) or more of the registered capital or voting stock or the power to appoint the general manager, factory chief or other principal person in charge of an enterprise or other entity.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise provided herein, the following words and terms used in this Contract shall have the meanings set forth below: Article 2 "Affiliate" means, in relation to Party A, any enterprise or other entity which, directly or indirectly, controls, or is controlled by, Party A; the term "control" meaning ownership of twenty-five percent (25%) or more of the registered capital or voting stock or the power to appoint the general manager, factory chief or other principal person in charge of an enterprise or other entity.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Affiliate' in relation to Party A is changed to specify that control means ownership of twenty-five percent (25%) or more of the registered capital. This creates an in-text contradiction with Article 2's very next paragraph which states, 'Affiliate' means, in relation to Party B, any company which, through ownership of voting stock or otherwise, directly or indirectly, is controlled by, under common control with, or in control of, Party B; the term 'control' meaning ownership of fifty percent (50%) or more of the voting stock or the power to appoint or elect a majority of the directors or the power to direct the management of a company. This now provides two different threshold for what makes an affiliate of each party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Affiliate" means, in relation to Party B, any company which, through ownership of voting stock or otherwise, directly or indirectly, is controlled by, under common control with, or in control of, Party B; the term "control" meaning ownership of fifty percent (50%) or more of the voting stock or the power to appoint or elect a majority of the directors or the power to direct the management of a company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the definition of 'Affiliate' in relation to Party A by changing the control threshold to 25%, while the 'Contradicted Text' defines 'Affiliate' in relation to Party B with a 50% control threshold. This creates a direct and significant in-text contradiction because the same term, 'Affiliate,' is defined differently for each party within the same contract, leading to ambiguity and potential disputes about which definition applies in various situations. This is a strong contradiction under the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Joint Venture Company may sell the Joint Venture Products in the domestic and overseas markets, and may entrust Third Party's to sell the Joint Venture Products with approval from the Board of Directors.
perturbation.changed_text: The Joint Venture Company may sell the Joint Venture Products in the domestic market only, and may entrust Third Party's to sell the Joint Venture Products with approval from the CEO.
perturbation.explanation: The change restricts the Joint Venture Company's sales to the domestic market only. This change, as well requires the approval of the CEO rather than the Board of Directors. This creates an in-text contradiction with Article 49, which states 'It is the intention of the parties that no less than 50% of the Joint Venture Products should be sold overseas.' This perturbation directly contradicts that article.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is the intention of the parties that no less than 50% of the Joint Venture Products should be sold overseas.
perturbation.justification: The changed text restricts the Joint Venture Company's sales to the domestic market only, directly contradicting the original intention stated in the 'Contradicted Text' that 'no less than 50% of the Joint Venture Products should be sold overseas.' This creates a clear and impactful contradiction that makes the contract unenforceable and inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: All employees hired by the Joint Venture Company must complete satisfactorily a six-month probationary period of employment before they will be considered regular employees of the Joint Venture Company.
perturbation.changed_text: All employees hired by the Joint Venture Company must complete satisfactorily a three-month probationary period of employment before they will be considered regular employees of the Joint Venture Company.
perturbation.explanation: The change reduces the probationary period from six months to three months. This affects the employee status and benefits eligibility timing, thus creating an in-text contradiction with Article 77, which states, 'The wages, welfare, labor insurance and other rights and obligations of working personnel and Management Personnel shall be regulated through individual or group labor contracts.' This article implies a standard duration for rights and obligations based on the original six-month period, making it unclear how labor contracts should be interpreted with a shorter probationary period.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Joint Venture Company shall adopt a labor contract system. The wages, welfare, labor insurance and other rights and obligations of working personnel and Management Personnel shall be regulated through individual or group labor contracts.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the probationary period from six months to three months. This directly impacts the 'rights and obligations' mentioned in the contradicted text, as the timing of when an employee becomes eligible for certain benefits and protections under labor contracts is altered. This creates a strong inconsistency because the labor contract system established in the contradicted text would be predicated on the initial six-month period, making it unclear which duration governs the employment terms.
